news.title: Delhi HC allows authorities to proceed with demolition drive in Shalimar Bagh
news.para: In a significant judicial decision, the Delhi High Court has authorized the widening of a road in Shalimar Bagh, allowing residents until May 30, 2026, to relocate. This development is vital for improving roadway efficiency and facilitating swift access for emergency services. The court highlighted that the advantages of public infrastructure advancements take precedence over private land claims.

news.title: Mangaluru civic body logs record ₹149.6 crore property tax collection in FY26
news.para: Mangaluru City Corporation achieved a record Rs 149.6 crore in property tax collection for 2025-26, exceeding its target. This success, driven by physical property inspections in pilot wards and campaigns, will fund development works. The corporation also plans to implement QR codes on advertisements and an online booking platform to enhance revenue.

news.title: Prayagraj civic body extends property tax relief to new buildings
news.para: Prayagraj Municipal Corporation will offer a 25% tax rebate on the annual rental value for newly constructed residential buildings. This benefit, aimed at providing financial relief and encouraging tax compliance, applies only to self-occupied, purely residential properties. The move aligns with previous demands for tax concessions for older buildings as well.

news.title: Telangana: Despite merger, building approval revenue in CURE sees only marginal rise
news.para: Despite merging municipalities into three corporations, revenue from building approvals in Telangana's Core Urban Region saw only a marginal increase. While the expanded region recorded ₹1,172 crore in 2025-26, this marks a significant decline from 2022-23. Most approvals now occur outside GHMC limits, with Cyberabad Municipal Corporation contributing the largest revenue share.

news.title: TIDCO colonies in Vijayawada showcase new township model
news.para: Andhra Pradesh is transforming urban housing for the economically weaker sections by developing integrated "mini townships" under APTIDCO. These projects now prioritize complete habitability, incorporating robust physical infrastructure like CC roads, underground drainage, and efficient water supply. The initiative also focuses on economic sustainability through Livelihood Centres and social amenities.

news.title: SC orders municipal bodies to conduct survey of commercial misuse in residential areas
news.para: The Supreme Court has mandated municipal bodies nationwide, including the Jaipur Municipal Corporation, to survey residential areas used for commercial purposes and submit affidavits detailing violations by May 15, 2026. This directive follows a hearing on unauthorized commercial use, aiming to curb widespread violations attributed to lax enforcement and a coordination gap between planning and licensing authorities.

news.title: Greater Noida looks for company to build logistics park in Dadri
news.para: Greater Noida Industrial Development Authority is appointing a developer for a 174-acre multi-modal logistics park in Kappa-II. Strategically located near Noida International Airport and freight corridors, the facility aims to boost the region's industrial hub status and reduce logistics costs for businesses.

news.title: Ghaziabad development body now requires tree plantation for all new plot approvals
news.para: Ghaziabad Development Authority has mandated tree plantation based on plot size to enhance the city's green cover. New rules require land earmarked for planting to be shown on maps for approval, with security deposits refunded upon satisfactory plantation. This initiative aims to combat severe air pollution, as Ghaziabad was recently identified as India's most polluted city.

news.title: Chandigarh administration clears FAR hike to cut flat prices, allow six floors
news.para: Chandigarh administration has increased the permissible Floor Area Ratio (FAR) to 2.4 for the Sector 53 housing project, allowing buildings up to six floors. This move aims to boost housing affordability by increasing the number of dwelling units and reducing per-unit costs. The revision is expected to soften prices for buyers, with further relaxations under consideration for other areas.

news.title: Jharkhand RERA imposes ₹83.5 lakh penalty on 77 builders over report delay
news.para: Jharkhand Real Estate Regulatory Authority (JRERA) has fined 77 builders a total of Rs 83,50,000 for failing to submit mandatory quarterly progress reports and ignoring court summons. JRERA chairman warned of bank account freezes if dues are not cleared, emphasizing accountability for over 50% of registered projects in non-compliance.

news.title: Cluster redevelopment scheme to benefit seven lakh slum dwellers: Maharashtra CM
news.para: Maharashtra Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has greenlit a slum cluster redevelopment scheme poised to benefit seven lakh residents. The state housing policy, 2025, will guide this integrated development. Significant funding was also approved for the Mata Ramabai Ambedkar Nagar and Kamaraj Nagar project, alongside a memorial at Ghatkopar. Additionally, BMC engineers and staff will be absorbed by the SRA.

news.title: Maharashtra CM clears acquisition of BSNL, MTNL land in Borivali for PMAY housing
news.para: Maharashtra's Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has given the green light for the purchase of land from BSNL and MTNL in Borivali. Spanning 28.84 acres, this property will pave the way for over 1,000 housing units under the Pradhan Mantri Awas Yojana, with the acquisition amounting to Rs 729 crore.

news.title: Ashiana Housing buys 28.55 acre land in Pune to build senior living homes
news.para: Ashiana Housing has acquired 28.55 acres of land in Pune for its largest senior living project to date. The development is expected to generate an estimated revenue of Rs 1,800 crore, catering to significant demand for senior homes in the region. This strategic acquisition follows a period of strong sales growth for the company.

news.title: Vedanta questions evaluation metrics behind selection of Adani's bid for JAL
news.para: Vedanta Group questioned lenders' evaluation metrics for Jaiprakash Associates Ltd (JAL), alleging their chosen bid from Adani Enterprises was lower. Vedanta argued their bid offered higher gross and net present value, and the evaluation process did not prioritize value maximization as per insolvency framework objectives.

news.title: Mumbai civic body MCGM to deploy AI system to overhaul real estate approvals
news.para: Mumbai's Municipal Corporation is set to deploy an advanced AI-driven system to streamline real estate approvals, aiming to expedite project clearances and enhance transparency. This initiative will integrate multiple civic processes onto a unified digital platform, reducing manual intervention and improving data-driven decision-making for developers and authorities.

news.title: Chandigarh administration set to notify bed and breakfast policy
news.para: Chandigarh is set to formalize its booming Bed & Breakfast sector with a new policy, aiming to prevent the conversion of homes into mini-hotels. The 'Bed & Breakfast Policy 2026' will enforce strict regulations, ensuring B&Bs maintain a residential appearance and avoid commercial activities beyond guest services.

news.title: Jaiprakash Gaur backs Adani Group's acquisition of JAL assets
news.para: Jaypee Group founder Jaiprakash Gaur has expressed full confidence in the Adani Group's leadership to take over his insolvent firm, JAL. This statement follows the Committee of Creditors' selection of Adani Enterprises' bid, a decision Gaur respects despite a challenge from rival bidder Vedanta. Gaur believes Adani will carry forward JAL's legacy with renewed energy and responsibility.

news.title: SC orders Nowhera to surrender in a week, links bail to sale deeds execution
news.para: The Supreme Court has ordered Heera Group chief Nowhera Shaik to surrender within a week, warning of a non-bailable warrant if she fails to comply. Her bail will depend on her cooperation in executing 16 pending sale deeds for auction purchasers, a process crucial for resolving a Rs 10,000 crore fraud involving thousands of investors.

news.title: Nagpur civic body plans offer up to 15% rebate as 44% property owners avail tax sops
news.para: The Nagpur Municipal Corporation (NMC) plans to reintroduce property tax rebates of up to 15% following last year's success, where nearly 44% of property holders availed the incentive. The proposed structure aims to maximize early collections and promote digital payments, offering 15% and 10% rebates for payments by June 30, 2026.
